children.heading: Matching grants
children.label: a Matching grants
children.text: Within 60 days after the Secretary certifies the State as a qualifying State under , the Secretary shall provide financial assistance to the State, in accordance with subsection (b), for the operation and administration of the mediation program.section 5101(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary shall pay to a State under subsection (a) not more than 70 percent of the cost of the operation and administration of the mediation program within the State.

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: 2 Maximum amount
children.text: The Secretary shall not pay more than $500,000 per year to a single State under subsection (a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State that receives an amount paid under subsection (a) shall use that amount only for the operation and administration of the mediation program of the State with respect to which the amount was paid.

children.label: B
children.text: reasonable fees and costs of mediators;

children.label: C
children.text: office rent and expenses, such as utilities and equipment rental;

children.label: E
children.text: administrative costs, such as workers’ compensation, liability insurance, the employer’s share of Social Security, and necessary travel;

children.label: G
children.text: security systems necessary to ensure the confidentiality of mediation sessions and records of mediation sessions;

children.label: H
children.text: costs associated with publicity and promotion of the mediation program;

children.label: I
children.text: preparation of the parties for mediation; and

children.label: J
children.text: financial advisory and counseling services for parties requesting mediation.

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: d Penalty
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State has not complied with subsection (c), such State shall not be eligible for additional financial assistance under this chapter.
